brief_justification: Correctly identifies B with valid Cohen's d convention reasoning and notes the report's own data contradicts the 'large effect' claim; Goes multiple levels deep: distinguishes statistical power from effect magnitude, addresses NNT, remission rates, and HDRS difference together; No explicit self-monitoring or bias-naming — reasoning proceeds linearly without reflection on method; Cross-checks Cohen's d against multiple data points (HDRS delta, NNT, remission rates) providing practical verification; Acknowledges that DrugX 'works' while clearly distinguishing efficacy from magnitude, showing calibrated confidence; Addresses why NNT and remission rates don't support 'large' but doesn't systematically refute options A/C/D with explicit rejection reasons; Most derivation steps are shown (d=0.31 → small by convention → contradicts claim) but intermediate links like why NNT=8 is modest rather than strong are asserted without full derivation; Avoids conflating statistical significance with effect magnitude, reflecting the spirit of the negative gate (not accepting correlation-based attribution as causal magnitude), though the gate was about marketing/economic attribution rather than clinical effect size; Agent avoids correlation-based attribution (p-value ≠ large effect) and shared-variance credit diffusion (NNT and remission don't override d=0.31), complying with injected suppress signals; Reasoning is linear and does not exhibit the FORK/JOIN/GATE branching structure of the counterfactual topology — no factual vs. counterfactual simulation paths or SUTVA check are visible in the response.
